channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Google employee accused of making $1 million from insider trading on Polymarket
item.description: Who could have seen this coming?

item.title: Quordle hints and answers for Thursday, May 28 (game #1585)
item.description: Looking for Quordle clues? We can help. Plus get the answers to Quordle today and past solutions.

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Thursday, May 28 (game #1082)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Thursday, May 28 (game #816)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: A Google employee allegedly used inside information to win $1.2 million on Polymarket
item.description: Federal prosecutors charged a Google employee with fraud after he allegedly made $1.2 million on Polymarket bets related to Search-related trends in 2025, as reported earlier by ABC News. In their now-unsealed complaint, prosecutors allege that Michele Spagnuolo "knew the outcome of these wagers before the trading public did because he had accessed Google's confidential, […]...

item.title: Google Security Engineer Arrested in Million-Dollar Polymarket Trading Scheme
item.description: According to federal prosecutors, Michele Spagnuolo made more than $1 million on the prediction market platform using confidential information about Google Search traffic.

item.title: 'Born from basalt' — How a tiny Hawaii startup is rewriting the rules of naval shipbuilding with 3D printers and 100% recyclable materials
item.description: Hawaii startup tests 3D printed basalt reinforced boats, aiming to replace traditional shipyards with distributed rapid naval manufacturing systems globally

item.title: What do you get with Meta and Instagrams new $3.99 subscription plan?
item.description: Meta is rolling out paid tiers across its apps, including Instagram, Facebook, and WhatsApp. Here's what's included.

item.title: Quote of the day by Google co-founder Larry Page: "When you aim for the stars you may come up short, but still reach the moon" — comments on the scale of ambition
item.description: The legendary tech entrepreneur outlines a philosophy at the heart of Google's meteoric rise in the last few decades

item.title: 'Self-reporting is the best way we can get this information out to the public': Erin Brokovich's next crusade is tracking new data centers across the US — and she wants your help
item.description: Erin Brockovich launches public reporting platform tracking environmental concerns surrounding AI data centers across the US.

item.title: A mega World Cup event combined with America’s 250th birthday will descend upon D.C. this summer
item.description: The National Mall will be the site of a FIFA Fan Zone, a giant Ferris wheel, performances by Flo Rida, and a whole lot of Trump-style theatrics. Washington, D.C., might not be hosting a World Cup match this year, but that doesn’t mean it’s not planning to throw a massive party.
